ConditionTarget.description:
Whether a condition attaches to a 3D body or a 2D boundary.

A fixed vocabulary, so it is an enum rather than a bare ``"body"`` /
``"boundary"`` string (doc 06, "no magic words"): autocomplete shows the
legal values and a typo is a static error.
ConditionTarget.title: ConditionTarget

Convection.description:
Convective (Newton-cooling) boundary condition (thermal, boundary target).

Carries both the heat-transfer coefficient *and* the external temperature it
exchanges with — together they fully specify the boundary, which a lone
coefficient could not.

ext_temperature.description: External (ambient) temperature (K) the boundary exchanges with.
ext_temperature.title: Ext Temperature

Convection.title: Convection

FixedTemperature.description: Dirichlet temperature boundary condition (thermal, boundary target).

value.description: Fixed temperature (K) imposed on the boundary.
value.title: Value

FixedTemperature.title: FixedTemperature

HeatFlux.description: Prescribed heat flux boundary condition (thermal, boundary target).
HeatFlux.title: HeatFlux

Magnetization.description:
Permanent-magnet body force (magnetostatics, body target).

The *direction* is per-region (in a Halbach array the same N52 block points
N/E/S/W depending on its slot) and lives here; the *magnitude*
``|M| = Bᵣ/μ₀`` is a material property and is passed into ``to_elmer`` by the
generator. ``direction`` need not be a unit vector — ``to_elmer`` normalizes
it (via :meth:`Vec3.normalized`) before scaling, so authoring an
axis-aligned ``Vec3(0, 1, 0)`` or a raw ``Vec3(0, 2, 0)`` gives the same
result.

direction.description: Magnetization direction (normalized before use); magnitude comes from the material.
direction.title: Direction

Magnetization.title: Magnetization

Physics.description:
Which physics preset the sif writer wires.

The string values match the keys of ``PHYSICS_PRESETS`` in ``elmer.sim`` and
the historical ``physics="magnetostatics"`` spelling, so existing configs and
serialized data keep working — but callers now pass ``Physics.MAGNETOSTATICS``
and get IDE completion + validation.
Physics.title: Physics

description:
A per-region override matched to a mesh entity by name.

Carries information that is not a material property but still needs to reach
the solver for a specific region, as a list of composable, self-describing
`conditions` (doc 02): a `Magnetization` body force for a magnet block, a
`FixedTemperature` boundary, etc. Each condition knows its physics, its
body/boundary target, and how to emit its own Elmer keywords — so a region
carries only the conditions it actually has, with no flat bag of optional
scalars.

conditions.description: Composable, self-describing conditions (body forces / boundary conditions) this region carries.
conditions.title: Conditions

title: EntityTag
